question: Design a base class `Publication` with private attributes `title` (string) and `price` (double). Implement methods to set and display these. From `Publication`, derive `Book` (adds private attribute `author` - string) and `Magazine` (adds private attribute `issueNumber` - int). Demonstrate creating objects of `Book` and `Magazine` and displaying their complete details.

test_cases.expected:
--- Book Details ---
Title: The C++ Programming Language
Price: $59.99
Author: Bjarne Stroustrup

--- Magazine Details ---
Title: C++ Magazine
Price: $9.50
Issue Number: 123

--- Another Book Details (using setters) ---
Title: Effective C++
Price: $45.00
Author: Scott Meyers

errors: The 'display' methods in both 'Book' and 'Magazine' derived classes fail to call 'Publication::display()'. This results in only the derived class's specific attributes (author, issueNumber) being displayed, omitting the base class attributes (title, price) and thus providing incomplete details as required by the question.
